researchFindings: Warcraft canon supports Andorhal as Lordaeron's agricultural distribution hub and an early plague-spread point; the chapter's grain-logistics focus is accurate.
Canon supports Jaina as Antonidas's apprentice and agent-level investigator before the fall of Lordaeron; her Dalaran-linked authority reads clean.
Canon supports pre-undeath Sylvanas as Ranger-General of Silvermoon: proud, tactical, dry, and direct. No corrections needed.
No external-canon conflict found in using Dalaran correspondence and local ward concerns as pressure points inside Andorhal's bureaucracy.
Internal story-canon guardrail preserved: the chapter close stays on operational dependence, not emotional softness.

observations.observation: Andorhal's role as a breadbasket and distribution city matched canon, so the logistics-heavy setting stayed intact.
Jaina's Antonidas connection and quasi-official Dalaran authority matched canon; no lore fix needed.
Pre-undeath Sylvanas voice stayed within Ranger-General guardrails; no banshee-era drift needed correction.
No geography contradiction surfaced in Andorhal's granary-district framing; research confirmed the city as a plague-spread node.
The real continuity risk was internal: the ending had to preserve operational trust without slipping into romance language.
The gate-entry block carried duplicate market detail and administrative texture; compressed it without losing the city's bureaucratic face.
Corin's introduction had multiple explanatory tail-lines after each beat; trimmed them to keep his frazzled competence sharper.
Havel's office sequence repeated the same proof from three angles; tightened it while keeping the sense of corroboration.
The assay-room explanation over-explained mechanism after the reader already had the answer; reduced the gloss without losing the plague logic.
The closing reflection had redundant self-annotation; cut it down and kept the hard 'no softness' frame.
Removed planning-language leakage in the narrator ('The chapter needed proof...').
Cut document-header speech in Corin's report ('Distribution irregularities') and replaced it with spoken urgency.
Reduced vague 'something' usage from three to one by naming the bureaucratic cover shape and Jaina's deeper-lock finding directly.
Reduced filter-word count from four to two by removing unnecessary 'saw/showed' perception scaffolding.
Removed the strict document-term hit in prose; no banned memo/lab vocabulary remains in narration.
Stripped extra narrator commentary after Sylvanas praising Corin; the praise lands harder without the footnote.
Trimmed repeated 'good/better/enough' commentary so Sylvanas reads decisive, not self-narrating.
Kept one brief atmospheric abstraction ('The room tightened.') because it carries tension cleanly and does not stack.
Negation scaffolding was reduced in Corin and Jaina's explanatory beats; the prose now commits faster.
Repeated institutional summaries around ledgers, routes, and functionaries were pared back so the investigation stays in-scene.
Audited every quoted line for document-register contamination and checked each replacement against Sylvanas/Jaina voice sheets.
Kept Jaina's gate-cover line in bureaucratic register on purpose because she is performing paperwork, not speaking naturally.
Kept Jaina's occult mage vocabulary ('preserving shell', 'carrier side', 'deeper lock') because it fits her voice sheet and is not modern lab jargon.
Sylvanas/Jaina split-scene dialogue stayed clipped and collaborative; no softening language was added.
Corin's spoken report now sounds like a strained young mage-clerk talking fast, not a memorandum reading itself aloud.
Havel's dialogue stayed plain and workmanlike; no institutional jargon survived in his spoken lines.
The capture-plan argument kept two distinct voices: Sylvanas presses action, Jaina presses proof.
The final banter beat ('It's a gift.') stayed dry and short; anything warmer would have violated the chapter's current relationship phase.
Meta-audited the densest prose blocks again after revision: assay reveal, one-machine synthesis, and final reflection.
Wordcount landed at 3856 from 4811, a reduction of 955 words while preserving all chapter beats.

changes.before: A clerk in brown wool sat at a table beside the gatehouse, pressing wax seals with a speed that suggested long practice and no imagination.
changes.after: A clerk in brown wool pressed wax seals.

changes.before: it sounded exactly like the kind of thing a busy office would prefer not to question
changes.after: it sounded exactly like the kind of thing a busy office would rather pass along than examine

changes.before: "Enough to know something's wrong. Not enough to make anyone useful believe it's wrong in the right direction."
changes.after: "Enough to know the books are lying. Not enough to make anyone useful believe it."

changes.before: The institution turned tangible around him: keys, seals, drawers, shelf codes, signatures, stamped notices. Dalaran's higher machinery might have swallowed his work whole. On the ground, the boy himself was excellent.
changes.after: Corin got them through the next doors quickly... On the ground, the boy was excellent.

changes.before: The chapter needed proof that the line held. Not a fresh theory every ten miles.
Havel showed her an outbuilding used for damaged sacks.
changes.after: Havel led her to an outbuilding for damaged sacks.

changes.before: "A preserving shell laid into the outer dust of the grain... After that the body does the expensive part for them."
changes.after: "A preserving shell worked into the outer dust... After that the body finishes the work."

changes.before: "This isn't contamination improvised by zealots. It's built like serious research. Layered. Controlled."
changes.after: "This isn't zealots smearing rot into grain and praying. It's layered. Controlled."

changes.before: There it was. An answer she had earned out of sight, handed back in language that did not ask Sylvanas to admire it, only to act on it.
changes.after: Removed; action resumed on the answer instead of annotating it.

changes.before: That got the faintest shift at the corner of her mouth. Gone at once. Enough.
changes.after: Removed; the dry exchange stands on its own.

changes.before: Jaina had done that cleanly, out of sight, and brought back an answer fit to move on. No haze left in it. No need to stand over her shoulder and make it smaller.
changes.after: Jaina had done that and brought back an answer fit to move on.

flags: Line 13: 'Dalaran correspondence pending local verification' kept — intentional cover-story bureaucratic phrasing in dialogue.
Lines 203-237: 'preserving shell' / 'carrier side' / 'deeper lock' kept — occult mage vocabulary aligned with Jaina's voice sheet, not modern lab jargon.
Ending guardrail preserved — operational trust only; no softening rewrite allowed in the final reflection.
